rows.description: Computus Thoma Hinton, Rectoris, a 2d. die Novr. 1788 ad eundem diem resolutum 1789.
rows.notes: Page title at top. Handwriting clear and spans full width; no numerical columns on this line. Very legible year and names.
Section heading below title. Word is slightly ambiguous (handwritten 'Arrearagia' / 'Arrearagia' / 'Arreariagia'), but clearly a heading and contains no amounts.
Description legible enough as a place/person and 'pro annus'. Amount columns well aligned; reads '05 19 0' clearly.

Description readable as 'Tintinhull Napier'. Amount appears as '05 1 1 1/4' — pence fraction looks like a small 'q' or '¼' after the 1; chosen as 0.25. Fraction recognition is moderately uncertain due to small loop.

rows.description: Tintinhull olim Hobell and Saml. Beeton
rows.notes: Description reads as a longer name entry referencing Tintinhull and Beeton; some letters uncertain ('olim Hobell') but overall entry clear. Amount clearly '10 6 0'.
Large amount clearly aligned in pounds column as '113 16 0'. 'S. Newington Rect.' description legible; numbers unambiguous and validate correctly.
Shortened description 'S. New. Tenem. do.' appears as a related entry ('do.'). Amount '01 16 0' relatively clear though the abbreviation makes the description slightly ambiguous.
Place name reads as 'Berrington' (could be 'Berrington/Berrington'). Numbers read '04 12 11' with pence '11' clear and within valid range.

rows.description: Gwinear Rect. pro dim. an. admid.
rows.notes: Description 'Gwinear Rect. pro dim. an. admid.' reasonably clear. Amount reads '30 0 0' unambiguously and aligns with pounds column.
Description is somewhat unclear ('Nodln/Nudln Ric.'); interpreted as a name plus 'pro annus'. Numeric '04 0 0' is clear in columns.
Place/name uncertain (reads 'Tetherin' or similar). Amount '03 0 0' is clear and columns are well aligned.
Description is hard to read ('Lad Lane longus' tentative). Amount reads '09 6 8' but handwriting of description and some of the digits is moderately ambiguous.
Shortened entry 'Do.' indicates same category. Amount '06 13 4' readable though the first digit of pounds is slightly faded; column alignment supports reading.
Entry 'Greenhow Do.' readable. Amount '01 13 4' clear; digits align with ruling columns and pence within valid range.
Place appears as 'Bampton' (or 'Barrington/Bampton'); amount '07 0 0' is crisp and unambiguous.
Place name slightly uncertain ('Medenham' reading); numeric '06 13 4' clear but description ambiguity reduces confidence.

rows.description: Sir G. P. Turner (Leven??) pro annus
rows.notes: Proper name 'Sir G. P. Turner' readable; parenthetical text uncertain. Amount '06 10 10' reads clearly in columns.

rows.description: Kempston Hall (St. Mark Par.) pro 3 ann.
rows.notes: Description partly inferred ('Kempston Hall' variant and parish note). Amount '05 14 0' visible though some letters in description are indistinct.
Reads 'Bromley olim Keck'; 'olim' (formerly) is visible. Amount '02 16 8' clear and validates (shillings <20, pence <12).

Description reads as 'Garington Rect. & Tenem.' (could be 'Gardington/Garsington'). Amount appears as '18 14 5 1/2' — the pence has an obvious half-penny mark; fraction interpretation (0.5) is plausible but faint, so confidence is low.
College entry 'Coll. Hertf.' (Hertford) readable. Amount '40 0 0' clearly written in pounds column; large and unambiguous.

Description appears to be 'Coll. Jesn' (possibly 'Coll. Jesus' or similar). Amount is uncertain in the image: the characters could be '00 10 0' or another value; chosen '00 10 0' because '00 40 0' would violate shilling limits. Very low confidence due to faded/faint digits.

rows.description: Taylor de parva Few pro annus
rows.notes: Description 'Taylor de parva Few' (parva = small) readable though somewhat old Latinized. Amount reads '05 0 8' clearly in columns.
Name looks like 'Wild Anish' (could be variant spelling). Amount '05 0 0' is clear.

Final total line at bottom reads 'Summa ann. receptor.' Amount appears as '258 5 1 3/4' (pence = 1 with three-quarters). Fraction '3/4' interpreted as 0.75; alignment with prior amounts supports reading.
